signals.title: USD/CAD — Tariff Shock + Technical Inflection
signals.text: USD/CAD trades at 1.4101, testing a key technical inflection point as price pushes to new session highs. The move is driven by a direct trade shock following reports of new 50% U.S. tariffs on selected Canadian goods, which fundamentally weighs on CAD export competitiveness. This headline catalyst aligns with a significant 150bp Fed-BoC carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25%) and a crowded COT speculative short position in CAD (lev_net -93,515, long% 20.6%). While price remains below the SMA20 at 1.4154, the bear flattener regime in the U.S. yield curve supports sustained USD strength. Risk: A reversal in tariff rhetoric or a surprise hawkish shift from the BoC that forces a cover of the extreme CAD short.

signals.title: USD/JPY — 40-Year High + Options Skew Divergence
signals.text: USD/JPY has reached a new 40-year high at 163.02, trading well above its SMA20/50/200 cluster (162.05/160.62/157.46). Despite the breakout, the 25d Risk Reversal at -1.42 (Saxo) shows a moderate put skew, indicating that the options market is paying a premium for JPY protection rather than chasing the USD upside. This divergence occurs as JPY speculative positioning remains extremely short (lev_net -105,238), leaving the pair vulnerable to a disorderly unwind if the BoJ intervenes or if VIX clears 25. Currently, the 275bp Fed-BoJ carry differential (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 1.0%) sustains the bid, but the negative real rate of -0.52% in Japan suggests the accommodative stance is nearing its limit. Risk: Direct BoJ intervention or a spike in VIX above 25 that triggers a safe-haven JPY bid.

signals.title: GBP/AUD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry Divergence
signals.text: GBP/AUD is currently the primary bilateral G10 theme with a 0.896% spread between the weekly leader (AUD) and laggard (GBP). AUD outperformance (+0.424% avg) is supported by a 60bp carry advantage over the USD (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%) and a hawkish RBA bias, while GBP (-0.472% avg) faces a moderate negative CESI score of -8. Speculative positioning shows a divergence, with specs net long GBP (+28,278) while the currency underperforms, suggesting a potential long-unwind risk. Conversely, AUD specs are net long (+21,946), aligning with the current strength. Risk: A dovish shift in RBA meeting minutes or a surprise beat in UK employment data that reverses the G10 spread.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + CPI Catalyst
signals.text: NZD/USD trades at 0.5831, holding above its SMA20/50/200 cluster (0.5729/0.5804/0.5823) following a CPI report that supports further RBNZ holds at 2.5%. A sharp divergence exists as COT LF specs remain net short -29,582 (long% 15.2%) despite the RBNZ's hiking stance and a hawkish market bias. This positioning is an extreme outlier relative to the neutral CESI score (+1) and the currency's 1M gain of +1.33%. If the RBNZ maintains its hawkish trajectory, the crowded spec short faces an asymmetric squeeze risk toward 0.5900. Risk: RBNZ signals a pause in the hiking cycle, validating the existing spec short and breaking the SMA support cluster.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + CESI Weakness
signals.text: EUR/USD is trading at 1.1409, slightly above its SMA20 (1.1416) but remains structurally capped by the SMA50/200 at 1.1526/1.1644. The options market is pricing an elevated vol premium with IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.7% (VRP +2.4%), signaling defensive hedging ahead of the July 23 ECB meeting. This caution is justified by a strong negative CESI score of -15, indicating persistent Eurozone data misses relative to the USD's strong positive score of +48. The 150bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.25%) continues to favor the USD, keeping EUR/USD upside limited to technical retracements. Risk: A hawkish ECB surprise that narrows the real rate differential (+1.47% USD vs +0.03% EUR).
